issues: Draft says 'their first B.C. Junior Hockey League championship in eight years' — OCR confirms this, so that is fine.
Draft says 'swept the BCJHL playoffs without a loss in three series' — OCR confirms 13 consecutive wins in three series, so that is accurate.
Draft says 'Captain Trevor Dickie' led the team — OCR confirms Dickie is captain, accurate.
Draft says 'defeated Kelowna Packers 3-2 on March 24, 1987' — date is plausible (paper is March 25), OCR says 'Tuesday night', consistent.

Draft says 'The team advances to face the Peace-Cariboo League champions in a best-of-nine final series beginning April 1' — OCR says the series begins April 3 (not April 1). OCR text: 'will be played in Richmond beginning April 3'.
Headline in draft is 'Sockeyes sweep BCJHL playoffs' — OCR clearly shows 'Sockeyes sweep BCJHL playoffs' (with a decorative 'S'), this is correct.
Byline in draft is 'Elliott Pap' — OCR clearly shows 'By ELLIOTT PAP', this is correct.

The bottom portion of the OCR contains adjacent-column noise about a hockey alumni/legends game (Boldirev, Sedlbauer, Schmautz, Shack, Henderson, Cournoyer, Sittler, Mahovlich, Mikita, Perreault) — this is unrelated to the Sockeyes article and should not be referenced in the description.

Photo caption names Dean Rutledge (#20) and Rob Sumner — draft does not mention these players in the photo, but that is a minor omission rather than an error. The draft does not incorrectly describe the photo.

Draft mentions 'swept the BCJHL playoffs without a loss in three series' but the key scorers (Jason Phillips, Steve Jaques, Brian Kozak, Bill Hardy, Pat Mangold) are not mentioned — minor omission, not an error.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes edge Kelowna Packers 3-2 to sweep the BCJHL playoffs and claim their first B.C. Junior Hockey League championship in eight years.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Orland Kurtenbach's Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Kelowna Packers 3-2 on Tuesday, March 24, 1987, at Richmond Arena to claim their first BCJHL championship in eight years, completing 13 consecutive wins across three playoff series without a loss. Captain Trevor Dickie, a former New Westminster Bruin, expressed confidence in the team's national title chances, while key goals came from Jason Phillips (including a shorthanded marker) and Pat Mangold. The Sockeyes next face the Peace-Cariboo League champions in a best-of-nine series beginning April 3 in Richmond, with the Alberta Junior League champions to follow.
review.notes: The lower portion of the OCR is adjacent-column noise about a hockey legends/alumni game (Henderson, Cournoyer, Sittler, Mahovlich, Mikita, Perreault, etc.) and is unrelated to the Sockeyes article. The only factual error in the draft is the series start date: the draft says April 1, but the OCR clearly states April 3.
